title: carex × luteola
intro: Carex × luteola, also known as the golden sedge, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The golden sedge is believed to provide several health benefits, such as reducing swelling and pain, treating urinary tract infections, and supporting kidney function. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
